summary.hardware: NVIDIA H100 PCIe 80GB (Scaleway via NVIDIA Brev)

inv15_decision.reason: INV-15: judge role='critic' risk=0.90 > threshold=0.65 → dense prefill mandated

honesty_note: Real H100 measurement on 1x NVIDIA H100 PCIe 80GB via Scaleway/NVIDIA Brev (apohara-h100-bench2). Model: Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B (FP16, dense). HuggingFace transformers 5.8.1. 5 agents (retriever, reranker, summarizer, critic, responder). Mode 'contextforge': in 'baseline' each agent encodes the full ~4K shared context + its agent-specific suffix from scratch. In 'contextforge' the shared context is encoded once at the top of the run; each downstream non-critic agent then encodes ONLY its agent-specific suffix (the shared-prefix portion is treated as already-cached). When the JCRSafetyGate fires for the critic (use_dense=True), that agent re-encodes the full prefix from scratch — this is the INV-15 safety override. The peak-VRAM delta between baseline and contextforge reflects the architectural saving from not replicating the shared 4K context across the 4 non-critic agents. We do NOT use transformers' past_key_values reuse path: Qwen3.6-27B's qwen3_5 hybrid-attention layers do not support it (seq_len=0 reshape crash). vLLM 0.21.0 also does not yet recognize qwen3_5 model_type. Peak VRAM measured via pynvml direct queries. The architectural mechanism this measurement validates is the same one the ContextForge vLLM plugin productizes; the INV-15 safety gate decisions are recorded independently in the per-agent records.
